z3:
(let ((a!1 (unit-resolution (asserted (or (not c02_03) (not c04_03)))
                            (asserted c04_03)
                            (not c02_03))))
  (unit-resolution a!1 (asserted c02_03) false))
solver_explanation:
Reasons for the clauses in the proof
c04_03: r4c3 is sandwiched between identical neighbors in its row
(or (not c02_03) (not c04_03)): leaving both r2c3 and r4c3 unshaded would break uniqueness because they have the same symbol
answer:
The cell r4c3 is sandwiched between two identical symbols in its row, which means according to the Sandwich Lemma, it must remain unshaded.

Looking at column 3, both r2c3 and r4c3 have the same symbol. If we leave both of them unshaded, this would violate the Uniqueness Rule because you can't have duplicate symbols in a row or column without shading one of them.

Since r4c3 must stay unshaded due to the Sandwich Lemma, the only way to satisfy the Uniqueness Rule is to shade r2c3. This ensures that there are no duplicates in column 3 while maintaining all the rules of Hitori.
